content: Just as human language learners use deliberate reflexion (the reflective learning practice taught in high school curricula) to refine implicit grammatical intuition by contrasting correct and incorrect output, large language models can improve their self-reflection mechanisms by integrating structured, curriculum-style spaced reflexion cycles that pair error examples with correct solutions. This builds on the core insight of the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework by grounding its dynamic self-reflection memory system in the pedagogical principles of human reflective learning.
